title: Lecture Three Sign-Changing Mass Wall Context
summary: This equation context records the folding step that converts the boundary problem for two opposite-mass fermions into one Dirac equation on all of space with a sign-changing mass profile.
assumptions: The boundary condition can be encoded by folding the two half-space problems into one whole-space problem.
scope: Equation-level context for the sign-changing-mass Dirac equation.

source_anchors.notes: The boundary gluing is rewritten as one Dirac equation with a sign-changing mass.

representation: Equation context for the folded sign-changing-mass problem.

math_expressions.notes: Boundary condition gluing the two opposite-mass fermions.
The two bulk equations before folding.
Folded whole-space Dirac equation with a sign-changing mass profile.

symbols.meaning: folded spinor defined on all of space

retrieval_hints: Use when the answer must show how the boundary problem becomes a domain-wall problem.
